en.extract:
The white-headed pigeon (Columba leucomela) is a pigeon native to the east coast of Australia.


Taxonomy and systematics
The pigeon family is a group of stout-bodied birds with short necks and short slender bills with a worldwide distribution. The white-headed pigeon is in the genus Columba, the same as the Rock dove (C. livia) from which the domestic pigeon is derived. It has no subspecies.
The white-headed pigeon was originally classified as C. norfolciensis, but the 1953 discovery of an 18th-century painting indicated that the description was likely for a different bird on Norfolk Island, perhaps the Pacific emerald dove (Chalcophaps longirostris). It is most closely related to the Metallic pigeon (C. vitiensis).


Description
The white-headed pigeon draws its name from its distinctive white head, neck, breast, and belly. In males, the wings are slate grey, along with the tail. The upperparts are grey black, with a green or purple iridescence. The bill is pink to dark red, but with a yellow or white tip. The legs and feet are pink to red. Females appear similar to males, but their white coloration is less brilliant and more muddied. Juveniles are similar to females, but with a greyer head and darker gray underparts. Juveniles have greyer feet, legs, and bills, with the bill white tipped. Adults range from 38–42 cm (15–17 in) from beak to tail, and weigh around 420 g (15 oz).
The eye ring and legs are red and the eyes are pale orange or yellow.
Their call is loud and gruff sounding like "WHOO!" followed by a gruff inhalation sounding "uk" (repeated three times). Sometimes the call is a low "oom".


Distribution and habitat
The pigeon's habitat is from Cooktown, Queensland to southern New South Wales, with increasing numbers now found in eastern Victoria. It can commonly be found in tropical regions, subtropical rainforest, scrub, watercourses and street trees. Since colonisation of Australia, their numbers have decreased but they have thrived on the introduced Camphor laurel (Cinnamomum camphora).


Behavior and ecology
The white-headed pigeon's nest generally consists of scanty twigs and is usually placed high in canopy up to 18 metres.  It tends to lay one cream-white egg. Breeding is mostly from October to December.
These pigeons are often found alone, in pairs or in small flocks. They are very quiet and elusive. Their flight is swift and direct.


Feeding
This bird's diet consists of fruits and seeds, with its favourite food being the invasive camphor laurel (Cinnamomum camphora). White-headed pigeons will consume buds, flowers, leaves, and ripe and unripe fruits of the camphor laurel year-round. They feed in tree canopies alone or small groups, but may also feed on the ground from time to time. Larger groups may gather when feeding on camphor laurels, up to 100.  It also eats fallen grains in cornfields.


Status
Due to its large range, and an increasing population, the white-headed pigeon is considered a least-concern species.


References

Pizzey and Knight, "Field Guide to the Birds of Australia", Angus & Robertson, ISBN 0-207-19691-5
Trounsen and Trounsen, "Australian Birds: A Concise Photographic Field Guide, Cameron House. ISBN 1-875999-47-7.

The '''white-headed pigeon''' ('''''Columba leucomela''''') is a [[pigeon]] native to the east coast of Australia.

== Taxonomy and systematics ==
The pigeon family is a group of stout-bodied birds with short necks and short slender bills with a worldwide distribution.<ref>{{Cite journal |last1=Winkler |first1=David W. |last2=Billerman |first2=Shawn M. |last3=Lovette |first3=Irby J. |date=2020 |title=Pigeons and Doves (Columbidae), version 1.0 |url=https://birdsoftheworld.org/bow/species/columb2/cur/introduction |journal=Birds of the World |language=en |publisher=[[Cornell Lab of Ornithology]] |doi=10.2173/bow.columb2.01 |s2cid=216304444 |url-access=subscription}}</ref> The white-headed pigeon is in the genus [[Columba (bird)|''Columba'']], the same as the [[Rock dove]] (''C. livia)'' from which the [[domestic pigeon]] is derived.<ref>{{Cite journal |last1=Lowther |first1=Peter E. |last2=Johnston |first2=Richard F. |date=2020 |title=Rock Pigeon (Columba livia), version 1.0 |url=https://birdsoftheworld.org/bow/species/rocpig/cur/introduction |journal=Birds of the World |language=en |publisher=[[Cornell Lab of Ornithology]] |doi=10.2173/bow.rocpig.01 |url-access=subscription}}</ref> It has no [[subspecies]].<ref name=":0">{{Cite journal |last1=Baptista |first1=Luis F. |last2=Trail |first2=Pepper W. |last3=Horblit |first3=H. M. |last4=Boesman |first4=Peter F. D. |last5=Garcia |first5=Ernest |date=2020 |title=White-headed Pigeon (Columba leucomela), version 1.0 |url=https://birdsoftheworld.org/bow/species/whhpig1/cur/introduction |journal=Birds of the World |language=en |publisher=[[Cornell Lab of Ornithology]] |doi=10.2173/bow.whhpig1.01 |s2cid=242912903 |url-access=subscription}}</ref>

The white-headed pigeon was originally classified as ''C. norfolciensis'', but the 1953 discovery of an 18th-century painting indicated that the description was likely for a different bird on [[Norfolk Island]], perhaps the [[Pacific emerald dove]] (''Chalcophaps longirostris)''. It is most closely related to the [[Metallic pigeon]] (''C. vitiensis).''<ref name=":0" />

== Description ==
The white-headed pigeon draws its name from its distinctive white head, neck, breast, and belly. In males, the wings are slate grey, along with the tail. The upperparts are grey black, with a green or purple iridescence. The [[Beak|bill]] is pink to dark red, but with a yellow or white tip. The legs and feet are pink to red. Females appear similar to males, but their white coloration is less brilliant and more muddied. Juveniles are similar to females, but with a greyer head and darker gray underparts. Juveniles have greyer feet, legs, and bills, with the bill white tipped. Adults range from {{Convert|38-42|cm|in|abbr=on}} from beak to tail, and weigh around {{Convert|420|g|oz|abbr=on}}.<ref name=":0" />

The eye ring and legs are red and the eyes are pale orange or yellow.

Their call is loud and gruff sounding like "WHOO!" followed by a gruff inhalation sounding "uk" (repeated three times). Sometimes the call is a low "oom".

== Distribution and habitat ==
The pigeon's habitat is from [[Cooktown, Queensland]] to southern [[New South Wales]], with increasing numbers now found in eastern Victoria. It can commonly be found in [[tropical]] regions, [[subtropical]] [[rainforest]], [[Scrubland|scrub]], watercourses and street trees. Since colonisation of Australia, their numbers have decreased but they have thrived on the introduced Camphor laurel ''(''[[Cinnamomum camphora]]'')''.

== Behavior and ecology ==
The white-headed pigeon's [[Bird nest|nest]] generally consists of scanty twigs and is usually placed high in [[canopy (biology)|canopy]] up to 18 metres.  It tends to lay one cream-white [[Bird egg|egg]]. Breeding is mostly from October to December.

These pigeons are often found alone, in pairs or in small flocks. They are very quiet and elusive. Their [[flight]] is swift and direct.

=== Feeding ===
This bird's diet consists of fruits and seeds, with its favourite food being the invasive [[camphor laurel]] (''Cinnamomum camphora''). White-headed pigeons will consume buds, flowers, leaves, and ripe and unripe fruits of the camphor laurel year-round. They feed in tree [[Canopy (biology)|canopies]] alone or small groups, but may also feed on the ground from time to time. Larger groups may gather when feeding on camphor laurels, up to 100.<ref name=":0" />  It also eats fallen grains in [[Maize|cornfields]].{{Citation needed|date=August 2022}}

== Status ==
Due to its large range, and an increasing population, the white-headed pigeon is considered a [[least-concern species]].<ref>{{Cite web |title=White-headed Pigeon (Columba leucomela) - BirdLife species factsheet |url=http://datazone.birdlife.org/species/factsheet/22690215 |access-date=2022-08-04 |website=datazone.birdlife.org}}</ref>

zh.extract:
白头鸽（学名：Columba leucomela）是鸽形目鸠鸽科鸽属的一种鸟类。其最显著的特征为白色头部、颈部、胸部和腹部，与黑灰色翅膀和尾羽形成鲜明对比。白头鸽分布于澳大利亚东海岸，从昆士兰州库克镇延伸至新南威尔士州伊登，偶尔可见于维多利亚州东南部。


特征
白头鸽体长约38至42厘米，体重约420克。头部、颈部、胸部及腹部为白色，有时带轻微黄色调，两侧腹部和下尾覆羽呈石板灰色。背部为石板黑色，肩羽、臀部、上尾及次级飞羽覆羽具金属绿色或紫色光泽。喙颜色由淡红至深红不等，尖端为黄白色；腿和脚为粉红色至红色。雌鸟外形与雄鸟相似，但白色部位可能呈斑驳或浅灰色。幼鸟羽色整体更暗，头顶偏灰，下体深灰，与白色下巴、喉部及颈侧形成明显对比；喙为暗红棕色，尖端白色，腿和脚呈红灰色。
